title: A1: Getting started — your first discrimination report card
text: vector unit-level estimates — say, 97 discrimination point estimates across large U.S. firms — standard error. noisiest estimates look extreme purely chance, raw ordering mistakes imprecision evidence. sort units data actually support, report one honest interval, without overstating difference noise bear? “Estimates firm-level discrimination inherently noisy … report card summarizes evidence sorting firms small number grades.” — Kline, Rose & Walters (Kline et al. 2024) gradepath native-R replication companion paper. vignette runs full report-card workflow one conceptual call, unpacks just happened.

title: A1: Getting started — your first discrimination report card

Pull report card workflow tidy data frame export CSV. Read one signature figure — firm’s posterior gap interval, ranked coloured grade. title: A1: Getting started — your first discrimination report card
text: KRW pipeline three plain steps, equations required. Estimate. Take firm’s raw gap standard error put common precision scale via beta-GMM standardization (precision exponent β\beta ). Rank. Deconvolve prior distribution true gaps, shrink firm’s estimate toward get posterior, compare every pair firms posterior probability one outranks . Grade. Solve integer program sorts firms small number grades along information-reliability frontier, selecting grade count KRW baseline λ=0.25\lambda = 0.25. Grades sorting posterior evidence, ranking contest: grade label integer 1 … n carries superiority statement. one-level race gender core accepted within declared scope, numbers match paper (β=0.5095\beta = 0.5095 vs reported 0.510; λ=0.25\lambda = 0.25 KRW baseline).

title: A2: The grading workflow — from estimates to a report card
text: first stage puts every firm’s gap common precision scale. KRW model observed estimate θ=sβv\theta = s^{\beta} v, standard error ss enters estimated exponent β\beta rather assumed away. exponent beta-GMM estimate; lives prior’s metadata: gradepath lands β=0.5095\beta = 0.5095, matching 0.510 KRW report. value near 0.50.5 says precision scales close square root ss: noisier firms standardized strongly comparison made. rest pr$metadata (characteristic, carrier count, support points, deconvolution’s alpha) records stage’s bookkeeping.
code: pr <- get_prior(fit) round(pr$metadata$beta, 4)        # the precision exponent beta #> [1] 0.5095

title: A2: The grading workflow — from estimates to a report card
text: pr object carries second stage’s output. standardized estimates pipeline deconvolves prior distribution GG true gaps — backs distribution latent vv , blurred sampling noise, reproduce spread observed estimates. prior’s shape flexibly estimated data rather imposed. recovered prior grid (support) carrying mass (density): pr$mean centre recovered prior, pr$scale scale label ("r", r-scale) rather spread; spread density-weighted standard deviation support grid (spread computed chunk ). distribution next stage shrinks toward: encodes much true variation across firms data actually support, separated noise.
code: prior_df <- data.frame(support = pr$support, density = pr$density) head(prior_df) #>       support     density #> 1 0.000000000 0.001590074 #> 2 0.001341692 0.001596067 #> 3 0.002683385 0.001602082 #> 4 0.004025077 0.001608119 #> 5 0.005366769 0.001614178 #> 6 0.006708461 0.001620259 pr$scale                                  # scale label: "r" (the r-scale), not a number #> [1] "r" w      <- pr$density / sum(pr$density)    # normalise the grid mass spread <- sqrt(sum(w * (pr$support - pr$mean)^2))  # density-weighted SD of the prior c(centre = pr$mean, spread = spread) #>    centre    spread  #> 0.3243633 0.2205386

title: A2: The grading workflow — from estimates to a report card
text: prior hand, third stage produces firm’s posterior: combines firm’s noisy estimate prior get shrunken gap credible interval. columns compare estimate (raw gap) posterior_mean (shrunken gap). distance empirical Bayes work: firm measured imprecisely (large se) pulled harder toward prior centre, estimate carries less information; precisely measured firm barely moves. lower/upper columns credible interval report card display -.

title: A2: The grading workflow — from estimates to a report card
text: fourth stage turns 97 posteriors social-choice object. every ordered pair firms (,j)(, j) computes Πij=Pr⁡(θi>θj)\Pi_{ij} = \Pr(\theta_i > \theta_j), posterior probability firm ii’s true gap exceeds firm jj’s. result J×JJ \times J matrix: entry near 11 says posterior strongly orders row’s firm column’s firm; entry near 0.50.5 says data separate . companion pw$ids holds firm identifiers matrix order. standalone recomputation posterior fast one-liner, want : matrix Pi thing grade integer program reads. carries ranking-superiority statement kind — pairwise posterior order.

title: A2: The grading workflow — from estimates to a report card
text: fifth stage one solves. sorts firms small number contiguous integer grades {1,…,k}\{1, \dots, k\} solving integer program Pi, penalized λ\lambda: larger λ\lambda lowers separation threshold 1/(1+λ)1/(1+\lambda) yields , finer grades; smaller λ\lambda raises threshold pools units fewer, coarser grades. calls write : solve integer program 97 firms (minutes Gurobi), run . read already-solved assignment loaded fit instead: fit$selected_grade slot assignment chosen selected penalty, gp_grade_fit lambda = 0.25. Two firms land -extreme grade, 81 middle, 14 least-extreme — published 2 / 81 / 14. Grade 1 simply -extreme θ\theta block; label integer, verdict.
code: # Solve the whole path over a lambda grid (the engine the pipeline uses): path <- gp_grade_path(get_pairwise(fit),                       lambda_grid = c(0.25, 0.5, 1), control = gp_control())  # Or solve a single penalty in isolation: gp_grade(get_pairwise(fit), lambda = 0.25) table(get_grades(fit))            # the grade composition: 2 / 81 / 14 #>  #>  1  2  3  #>  2 81 14 fit$selected_grade$lambda         # the selected penalty #> [1] 0.25

title: A2: The grading workflow — from estimates to a report card
text: sixth stage reports cost compression. penalty λ\lambda grid yields one grade count, discordance rate (share comparable pairs grades run ) tau_bar (average -grade separation). frontier table — one row per penalty — cached solved path, reading instant: lambda = 0.25 reliability 1−DR≈0.961 - \text{DR} \approx 0.96 tau-bar ≈0.21\approx 0.21: three grades agree underlying posterior ordering 96% comparable pairs. Moving along λ\lambda trades grade count reliability — trade-frontier. plot , assemble gp_frontier object; fast read solve. call .data.frame() — read fit$grade_path$summary numbers, , use object plot: information-reliability frontier RACE fit (KRW Figure 6b): point one penalty lambda grid, plotted average -grade separation reliability (1 - discordance rate). selected lambda = 0.25 grading sits frontier.
code: s <- fit$grade_path$summary sel <- s[abs(s$lambda - 0.25) < 1e-8, ] sel[, c("lambda", "grade_count", "discordance_rate", "reliability", "tau_bar")] #>   lambda grade_count discordance_rate reliability   tau_bar #> 1   0.25           3       0.03869221   0.9613078 0.2069798 fr <- gp_frontier(fit$grade_path, pairwise = get_pairwise(fit)) gp_plot_frontier(fr)

what: 5. The accessors: the six get_* functions
title: A3: Reading and exporting results
text: gp_fit read accessor family, never reaching $ slots. accessor read-lookup recomputes nothing — reach one whose output downstream step needs. six. get_grades(fit) — selected grading named integer vector length 97, names equal firm ids. cbind() onto firm table pass table() composition: get_report_card(fit) — assembled gp_report_card object, source tidy table section 4. Reach want object prints plots, just rows: get_posterior(fit) — native gp_posterior (per-firm means, standard deviations, intervals). data frame carries estimate, se, id, label, posterior_mean, posterior_sd, lower, upper, scale, ; estimate versus posterior_mean shrinkage: get_prior(fit) — deconvolved native gp_prior, list carrying support, density, mean, scale, diagnostics, metadata. precision exponent β\beta — number cite show replication lands paper — lives metadata: gradepath lands β=0.5095\beta = 0.5095, matching 0.510 KRW report. get_pairwise(fit) — gp_pairwise social-choice input grading solved : J×JJ \times J matrix Πij=Pr⁡(θi>θj)\Pi_{ij} = \Pr(\theta_i > \theta_j) $matrix, firm order $ids: entry near 11 says posterior strongly orders row’s firm column’s; entry near 0.50.5 says data separate . matrix encodes pairwise posterior order carries superiority statement. get_control(fit) — exact gp_control() produced fit, reproducibility receipt: grid, backend, precision rule, interval level, solver options, seed, schema version, provenance:

title: A3: Reading and exporting results
text: Three exports cover almost every hand-. CSV. first analytical artifact: write tidy table CSV lands spreadsheet, paper table, co-author’s pipeline. command run machine (write package working directory ): RDS round-trip. second reproducible artifact: whole gp_fit, saved saveRDS(), round-trips exactly — every slot, prior, posterior, pairwise matrix, control come back identical. demonstrate live tempfile(), chunk runs proves integrity: TRUE guarantee rely ship solved fit alongside paper: collaborator readRDS()s file recovers identical object solved, re-solve drift. (precisely bundled parity fits travel inside package.) ASCII card file. third human-readable artifact: result-first card written text file, ready attach issue paste README. Like CSV, command run :
code: write.csv(as.data.frame(fit), "report_card_race.csv", row.names = FALSE) path <- tempfile(fileext = ".rds") saveRDS(fit, path) identical(fit, readRDS(path))   # a gp_fit round-trips through RDS exactly #> [1] TRUE writeLines(format_gp_report_card_cli(get_report_card(fit)), "card.txt")

title: A4: The figure cookbook
text: gradepath turns finished grading four pictures, four carry argument Kline, Rose & Walters (Kline et al. 2024). single verb returns ggplot, answers one visual question: report card — every firm sit, sure ? posterior-contrast heatmap — cleanly grade blocks separate? information-reliability frontier — compressing fewer grades cost, simpler scoring rules compare? conditional discordance matrix — grades cuts cleanest? cookbook gives figure four-part recipe can navigate need communicate: Purpose — one visual question answers. Lead chunk — lines prepare object plot. Default plot — figure shipped, reading guide. One variant — single argument worth knowing. Two things hold throughout. First, every gp_plot_*() verb returns ggplot renders nothing — print (code chunk automatically), can keep extending + theme_*() + scale_*. Second, nothing solves: load proven RACE fit build four figures . bundled 97-firm RACE report card — grades 2 / 81 / 14 KRW baseline lambda = 0.25, reliability 0.96, tau-bar = 0.21. Everything reads fit. reminder start drawing: grades sorting posterior evidence, never contest, grade 1 simply -extreme block — figures show structure, ranking verdict. one-level race core accepted within declared scope, figures descriptive.

title: A4: The figure cookbook
text: Purpose. Show every firm : shrunken estimate, uncertainty, grade landed . KRW Figure 7, headline figure. Lead. Pull assembled card fit get_report_card() hand verb. Default plot. horizontal point--interval (“caterpillar”): one firm per row, -extreme firm top. RACE report card (KRW Figure 7): 97 firms shown posterior White-minus-Black callback gap (point) credible interval (horizontal line), one firm per row, ranked -extreme firm top coloured grade 2 / 81 / 14 split. read . Three things carry meaning, reading order whole skill: Vertical position rank. Top row -extreme firm; eye travel top--bottom “least extreme.” Horizontal position posterior gap. point sits x-axis firm’s shrunken estimate — already pulled toward centre empirical Bayes, honest gap, raw one. Line length uncertainty. long horizontal line imprecisely measured firm; short one tightly measured firm. first thing eye catch intervals overlap: two firms’ lines cover ground, data separate , colour blocks respect exactly . colour grade. three colour bands stack top--bottom, grade boundaries read points colour changes — fall intervals stop overlapping, arbitrary cut-points. Variant — max_rows. 97 rows labels crowd; show top ranking extremes story. verb adds “Showing N M” subtitle truncation never silent. RACE report card restricted 20 -extreme firms (max_rows = 20): top ranking full height, subtitle noting 20 97 firms shown. one-argument turns: order = "grade" groups rows grade block instead rank, zero_line = "" always draws dashed reference zero (default "auto" draws intervals straddle zero, can see glance firms distinguishable gap ).
code: card <- get_report_card(fit) gp_plot_report_card(card) gp_plot_report_card(card, max_rows = 20)

title: A4: The figure cookbook
text: Purpose. Look structure behind grades: full matrix pairwise posterior contrasts, reordered grade blocks sit together. KRW Figure 5a. Figure 1 shows firms one time, shows every firm every . Default plot. verb reads pairwise matrix grades straight fit, lead chunk — pass fit. grade-sorted posterior-contrast heatmap RACE fit (KRW Figure 5a): 97 97 pairwise outranking matrix rows columns reordered grade grade blocks contiguous (grade 1 top-left), black segments separating blocks, cells reaching different-grade threshold outlined. read . Read grid light dark, block block: cell one ordered pair. shade posterior probability row firm extreme column firm — dark means “almost surely ordered way,” washed-middle grey means “data tell two apart.” diagonal runs corner corner, rows/columns sorted grade grade 1 top-left. picture read gradient: dark upper-right triangle (-extreme firms less-extreme ones), light lower-left. black segments mark grade boundaries. eye catch square blocks carve along diagonal: clean grading shows near-uniform shade inside block (firms grade hard separate) sharp contrast across blocks (different grades really different). outlined cells pairs reach different-grade threshold — evidence earned cut. structure plot, contest: dark cell says posterior orders one firm another, nothing . Variant — threshold = FALSE. Drop threshold outlines see raw contrast surface annotation — useful want shading alone tell story. posterior-contrast heatmap threshold = FALSE: grade-sorted outranking matrix shown raw shading, without red outlines cells reach different-grade threshold.
code: gp_plot_posterior_contrast(fit) gp_plot_posterior_contrast(fit, threshold = FALSE)

title: A4: The figure cookbook
text: Purpose. Show trade-frontier makes explicit: reliability (1 - DR, x-axis — often grades agree pairwise posterior ranking) information grades carry (tau-bar, y-axis — posterior expected rank agreement). finer grading (grades) carries information less reliable; pooling fewer grades raises reliability cost information. frontier traces trade-across grade path overlays simpler scoring rules, can see gradepath grading sits relative . KRW Figure 6b. Lead. frontier object. Build fit’s grade path pairwise, pass benchmark_scores — one value per firm, named firm id — overlay naive rules. overlay raw gap, full posterior mean, half-shrinkage rule. Default plot. Plot fr. figure carries two-row legend bottom, give slightly wider, shorter canvas (fig.width = 8, fig.height = 5.5) legend clipped. information-reliability frontier RACE fit (KRW Figure 6b): optimal-grade path traced reliability (1 - discordance rate, x-axis) posterior expected agreement tau-bar (y-axis), selected lambda = 0.25 grading marked three naive scoring rules overlaid dominated points. read . two axes whole idea, benchmarks payoff: x-axis reliability, 1 - DR — often grades agree underlying posterior ordering. right cleaner. y-axis tau-bar, posterior expected agreement carried grading. Higher informative. connected points frontier . Walk one end trading grade count reliability; highlighted point selected lambda = 0.25 grading, subtitle reports exact (1 - DR, tau-bar) coordinates. overlaid benchmark points simple scoring rules — raw gap, full shrinkage, half shrinkage. eye catch sit inside frontier: gradepath grading reaches reliability , figure’s point. Colour shape merged one two-row legend bottom, -plot text labels, read series single legend. benchmarks descriptive reference points, opponents. Variant — benchmarks = FALSE. Drop naive rules show bare frontier — trade-curve , benchmarks story. bare information-reliability frontier (benchmarks = FALSE): just optimal-grade path reliability tau-bar, selected lambda highlighted naive-rule overlays. small turns: annotate = FALSE drops coordinate readout subtitle cleaner caption, highlight = "" marks every path point instead selected one.
code: post <- get_posterior(fit) ids  <- get_pairwise(fit)$ids fr <- gp_frontier(   fit$grade_path, pairwise = get_pairwise(fit),   benchmark_scores = list(     raw_estimate     = setNames(post$estimate,       ids),   # the raw gap     posterior_mean   = setNames(post$posterior_mean, ids),   # full shrinkage     linear_shrinkage = setNames(0.5 * post$estimate, ids)))  # half shrinkage gp_plot_frontier(fr) gp_plot_frontier(fr, benchmarks = FALSE)

title: A4: The figure cookbook
text: Purpose. Ask grades cuts cleanest. pair grades reports discordance rate — posterior share cross-grade firm pairs whose grade ordering runs posterior ordering. KRW Figure 10a, reads fr object. Default plot. lower-triangular heatmap -grade discordance rates. conditional discordance matrix RACE fit (KRW Figure 10a): lower-triangular heatmap -grade discordance rate pair grades, -extreme grade columns (grade 1 left) less-extreme grade rows, cell annotated rate. Small -diagonal values mean clean cuts grades. read . small triangle, read every cell: cell one grade pair, -extreme grade columns (grade 1 left) less-extreme grade rows. cell value shade discordance rate pair — posterior share cross-grade firm pairs ordered posterior. Darker discordant. eye catch -diagonal cells light. light -diagonal means cut two grades clean: almost every cross-grade pair ordered way posterior orders . dark cell flag two adjacent grades data struggle keep apart. RACE fit -diagonal stays small — grades separate cleanly. Variant — annotate = FALSE. Drop printed values shading-heatmap, cleaner many grades want colour alone read. conditional discordance matrix annotate = FALSE: lower-triangular -grade heatmap shown shading , without numeric rate cell.
code: gp_plot_discordance(fr) gp_plot_discordance(fr, annotate = FALSE)

title: A4: The figure cookbook
text: Every gradepath verb already draws package house style, theme_gradepath() — clean theme built ggplot2::theme_minimal() top right spines removed, light horizontal grid, vertical grid, legend bottom. setup chunk made session default theme_set(theme_gradepath()), plots match four figures without extra call. apply explicitly two ways: set default, add single plot +. theme_gradepath() applied plain ggplot firm’s raw gap posterior gap: gradepath house style bottom legend, light horizontal grid, top right spine. verbs return ordinary ggplots, can also re-apply extend theme four figures — example gp_plot_discordance(fr) + theme_gradepath(base_size = 13) bump font slide.
code: ggplot(as.data.frame(fit), aes(estimate, posterior_mean)) +   geom_abline(linetype = 2, colour = "grey60") +   geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +   labs(x = "raw gap", y = "posterior gap", title = "RACE — raw vs posterior") +   theme_gradepath()

title: A4: The figure cookbook
text: four figures colour grades one shared ordinal scale, perceptually uniform, colour-blind-safe viridis ramp grade 1 (-extreme block) dark, high-contrast end. colour grade-keyed plot way, add scale_colour_gradepath() (spelled scale_color_gradepath() ) colour aesthetic, scale_fill_gradepath() fill. ordinal grade palette applied hand: 97 firms plotted posterior gap rank coloured grade scale_colour_gradepath(), custom plot uses exact grade colours report card. point shared scale consistency: firm’s grade reads colour custom plot report card, reader’s eye carries mapping across figures. fill aesthetic (tiles, bars) use scale_fill_gradepath(); pass n_grades fix palette length data contain every level. colour ordinal encoding evidence, never value judgement.
code: card <- as.data.frame(fit) ggplot(card, aes(sort_rank, posterior_mean, colour = factor(grade))) +   geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.85) +   scale_colour_gradepath(name = "grade") +   labs(x = "rank", y = "posterior gap")

title: A4: The figure cookbook
text: rather remember verb draws figure, ggplot2 autoplot() generic dispatches gradepath object — returns ggplot matching gp_plot_*() verb , without attaching ggplot2. autoplot() frontier draws frontier; report card draws caterpillar. autoplot() gp_frontier object: information-reliability frontier gp_plot_frontier(fr), dispatched ggplot2 generic. autoplot() report-card object: caterpillar gp_plot_report_card(get_report_card(fit)), dispatched ggplot2 generic. S3 methods, call autoplot(x) (autoplot.gp_frontier(x)) let dispatch pick figure. autoplot() also works bare fit — draws frontier — forwards extra arguments underlying verb, autoplot(get_report_card(fit), max_rows = 20) behaves like variant section 2.
code: autoplot(fr) autoplot(get_report_card(fit))

title: A4: The figure cookbook
text: Every verb returns ggplot, save four ggsave(). Pass plot object, filename, size. useful rule thumb canvases : report card wants tall aspect (many rows), heatmaps want square one, frontier wants wide one (two-row bottom legend). verify recipe end--end without leaving file working directory, write temporary path check exists: paper, swap tempdir() path filename reach vector device (device = cairo_pdf PDF) journal wants vector art.
code: # The report card: tall. ggsave("fig_report_card.png", gp_plot_report_card(get_report_card(fit)),        width = 7, height = 8, dpi = 150) # The heatmaps: square. ggsave("fig_posterior_contrast.png", gp_plot_posterior_contrast(fit),        width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 150) ggsave("fig_discordance.png", gp_plot_discordance(fr),        width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 150) # The frontier: wide, so the bottom legend is not clipped. ggsave("fig_frontier.png", gp_plot_frontier(fr),        width = 8, height = 5.5, dpi = 150) out <- file.path(tempdir(), "fig_frontier.png") ggsave(out, gp_plot_frontier(fr), width = 8, height = 5.5, dpi = 150) file.exists(out) #> [1] TRUE

See ?gp_report_card and Appendix A. #> i  full table: as.data.frame(card)   formatted CLI: format_gp_report_card_cli(card)

what: Seeded Monte-Carlo calibration of the one-level KRW grading method — gp_calibrate
title: Seeded Monte-Carlo calibration of the one-level KRW grading method — gp_calibrate
text: gp_calibrate() standalone, seeded, qualitative Monte-Carlo calibration harness. treats fitted prior ground truth, simulates n_sim synthetic datasets , re-runs full one-level pipeline (krw_report_card()) , checks grading method's frequentist guarantees hold qualitatively: empirical discordance rate (DR) tracks dr_target; posterior credible intervals cover truth near ci_level; empirical grading's regret oracle KNOWS true prior small non-negative. replication KRW Table E1 (parseable E1 artifact Matlab Monte-Carlo stream reproduced; see calibration scope m5-two-level--calibration vignette), seeded match Matlab (15238 default cosmetic homage; seed fixes -package reproducibility), headline result part default scorecard. never runs package load vignette render. result compact gp_calibration object: scalar summaries plus two boolean verdicts dr_ok / coverage_ok.

title: Seeded Monte-Carlo calibration of the one-level KRW grading method — gp_calibrate
code: gp_calibrate(   fit,   n_sim = 200L,   seed = 15238L,   dr_target = 0.05,   ci_level = 0.9,   min_ok = 1L )

title: Seeded Monte-Carlo calibration of the one-level KRW grading method — gp_calibrate
text: fit gp_fit krw_report_card() / gradepath() whose fitted prior, precision exponent beta (additive mu gender), observed standard errors s_i define data-generating truth. n_sim Integer; number Monte-Carlo synthetic datasets draw re-fit. Default 200L. Use small value (e.g. 5L) fast check; draw triggers one grade-IP solve. seed Integer; RNG seed -package reproducibility. Default 15238L (cosmetic homage reference seed – reproduce Matlab stream). seed yields identical output; caller's RNG stream restored afterward. dr_target Numeric [0, 1]; target discordance rate mean empirical DR compared dr_ok verdict (passes within 0.01). Default 0.05. ci_level Numeric open interval (0, 1); nominal credible-interval level, also used refit's reporting-interval level. coverage_ok verdict passes mean coverage within 0.02 . Default 0.90. min_ok Integer >= 1; minimum number successful (non-skipped) draws required harness reports. fewer draws succeed errors rather averaging noise. Default 1L.

title: Seeded Monte-Carlo calibration of the one-level KRW grading method — gp_calibrate
text: validated gp_calibration object (list class c("gp_calibration", "list")) summarizing run: n_sim, seed Integer; requested number draws RNG seed used. characteristic "race" "gender"; truth's demographic. n_ok, n_failed Integer; successful skipped draw counts (n_ok + n_failed == n_sim). dr_mean, dr_target Numeric [0, 1]; mean empirical discordance rate successful draws target compared . coverage, ci_level Numeric; mean theta-scale interval coverage successful draws nominal level compared . regret_mean Numeric; mean regret empirical grading versus true-prior oracle (non-negative construction). dr_ok, coverage_ok Logical; two qualitative verdicts (DR within 0.01 target; coverage within 0.02 ci_level). provenance Named list: producer, selected lambda, beta_truth, n_units, oracle coverage-scale tags, matlab_rng_parity = FALSE.

title: Seeded Monte-Carlo calibration of the one-level KRW grading method — gp_calibrate
text: harness robust refit non-convergence. draw's refit wrapped tryCatch; failed refit (e.g. singular step-1 GMM moment covariance) degenerate refit (implausibly large |beta| corrupts r-scale) skipped counted n_failed. reported means successful draws . fewer min_ok draws succeed, gp_calibrate() errors rather averaging noise. Two scale subtleties handled internally: ORACLE grading built known true prior WITHOUT re-deconvolving (pairwise comes gp_posterior_weights(prior = truth, ...)), gradings scored truth pairwise, regret non-negative construction. COVERAGE assessed beta-invariant theta scale (refit's theta-scale reporting interval true theta_i = s_i^beta * v_i), stays coherent even refit's estimated beta differs truth's. Reproducibility: whole draw loop runs single state-preserving seeded context, seed yields identical results caller's global RNG stream left untouched. Cost: draw re-solves grade integer program , run costs n_sim refit--grade solves. function prints one-line budget heads-(ordinary, suppressible message()) loop whenever n_sim * <number units> exceeds 600; message informational never changes result. quick check use small n_sim. tiny weakly identified fits verdicts frequently FALSE – small-N KRW beta-GMM noisy r-scale can ill-identified – meaningful calibration needs well-identified fit (KRW's real multi-firm input). harness reports honestly either way; never tunes data force pass.

title: Compare a replicated value against the gradepath registry — gp_check
text: gp_check() single registry comparator used replication harness. looks one row gp_registry id, reads row's published paper value absolute tolerance, compares already--unit replicated value , returns one-row verdict data frame. harness embeds tolerances – every band comes registry – gp_run_all() vector driver calls per target.

title: Compare a replicated value against the gradepath registry — gp_check
text: id Character scalar; registry key present gp_registry$id. id registry raises error. replicated Scalar replicated value, already converted registry unit (e.g. pass percent rows whose unit "percent"). Defaults NA_real_, yields n/status (nothing compare). producer_status Character scalar; runtime status producing stage, normalized internally. Defaults "OK". non-OK status short- circuits numeric comparison returns status UNVERIFIED (producing stage acceptance-ready), regardless replicated. registry Registry data frame look id . Defaults NULL, uses bundled package data gp_registry. label Character scalar quantity label overriding registry$quantity returned row. Defaults NULL (use registry's quantity text).

title: Compare a replicated value against the gradepath registry — gp_check
text: one-row data.frame describing comparison. id registry id checked. quantity Human-readable target description (label supplied, else registry quantity). paper Numeric published value registry. replicated Numeric replicated value compared (NA producer status non-OK). delta replicated - paper (NA compared). tol Absolute PASS tolerance registry. unit, class, milestone registry row's unit, tolerance class, milestone, passed . status One PASS, FAIL, n/(missing value), -tol (missing tolerance), UNVERIFIED (non-OK producer status). reason Short reason string PASS (e.g. outside_tolerance, missing_value, missing_tolerance); NA PASS. producer_status normalized producer status used.

title: Create a gradepath run-control object — gp_control
code: gp_control(   lambda_grid = seq(0, 1, by = 0.01),   backend = getOption("gradepath.backend", "gurobi"),   precision_rule = c("none", "krw_gmm"),   interval_level = 0.9,   solver_options = NULL,   time_limit = NULL,   mip_gap = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Create a gradepath run-control object — gp_control
text: lambda_grid Numeric vector; frontier penalty values grade path solved . Must unique, strictly increasing, lie [0, 1], must contain parity anchors 0.25 (KRW's published selection) 1.00 (endpoint warm-start). Defaults reference grid seq(0, 1, = 0.01); passing NULL restores reference grid, later grade-path execution may narrow . backend Character scalar; planned grade-IP solver backend, one "gurobi", "highs", "glpk", "symphony". Defaults value getOption("gradepath.backend", "gurobi") – Gurobi parity backend (solver KRW used), "highs", "glpk", "symphony" license-free open alternatives ("highs" calls highs package directly; "glpk" "symphony" route ROI plugins). v1 "roi_highs" / "roi_glpk" / "roi_symphony" spellings accepted normalized short names. "auto"; backend availability checked solver call time, . precision_rule Character scalar; precision-handling rule. "none" keeps raw estimate (r) scale; "krw_gmm" selects gradepath-native KRW beta-GMM standardization. Defaults "none". interval_level Numeric scalar; credible-interval level stored downstream posterior report-card objects. probability open interval (0, 1). Defaults 0.90. solver_options Named list; backend-specific solver options. portable keys time_limit mip_gap (compatibility spellings max_time mip_rel_gap) range-checked place; key passes untouched. mip_gap honored Gurobi, HiGHS, SYMPHONY current GLPK route. Defaults empty list. time_limit Numeric scalar NULL; ergonomic top-level alias solver time limit seconds (finite, strictly positive), merged solver_options$time_limit. Supplying alongside time_limit (max_time spelling) inside solver_options error. Defaults NULL (time limit set ). mip_gap Numeric scalar NULL; ergonomic top-level alias relative MIP optimality gap [0, 1], merged solver_options$mip_gap. Supplying alongside mip_gap (mip_rel_gap spelling) inside solver_options error. Defaults NULL (gap target set ). seed Integer scalar NULL; non-negative seed stochastic (two-level Monte-Carlo) paths. Defaults NULL (unseeded).

title: Create a gradepath run-control object — gp_control

Portable solver runtime knobs may passed either inside solver_options via ergonomic top-level aliases time_limit mip_gap; supplying knob twice – including compatibility spelling alongside ergonomic alias – error.

title: Summarize the frontier of a solved grade path — gp_frontier

Supplies penalty grid per-penalty grade assignments frontier built . pairwise Optional gp_pairwise (gp_pairwise() get_pairwise()). NULL (default) gp_frontier() returns frontier table cached gp_grade_path(); benchmarks conditional discordance matrix left empty. Supplying must match grade_path$ids unlocks . ids must equal grade_path$ids. selected_lambda Optional numeric penalty conditional discordance matrix evaluated. NULL (default) uses grade_path$selection$selected_lambda; value supplied must match penalty grid exactly. benchmark_scores Optional named list (one-row-per-unit data frame) per-unit scores naive baselines built . Requires three numeric vectors named raw_estimate, posterior_mean, linear_shrinkage, one value per unit named (column-aligned ) unit ids pairwise$ids; reordered id order. , five baseline grade scorings formed – rank cut three vectors plus decile quartile bins posterior_mean – scored pairwise profile. Defaults pairwise$control. selected_lambda Numeric scalar grid; penalty recorded default reportable selection returned path. Defaults 0.25 (KRW's published selection). Recording never triggers solve. selection_rule Character scalar; label stored alongside selected_lambda path's selection metadata. Defaults "baseline_lambda_0.25". acceptance_mode Logical; solver solution-quality policy, honored gurobi backend (open backends solve single invocation, ignored ). FALSE (default) keeps first Gurobi path returns normalized result, selected solve stops gap_reached / time_limit reported honestly status (lands UNVERIFIED downstream). TRUE additionally attempts later Gurobi paths prove optimum earlier path acceptance-ready; stays honest never relabels non-optimal solve optimal. chosen value recorded fit's backend$acceptance_mode.

title: Pairwise outranking probability matrix — gp_pairwise
text: object gp_fit (read-accessor path), list returned gp_posterior_weights() carrying $W (J x M) $reporting_support (M x J), bare J x M W matrix. bare matrix accepted reporting_support supplied shared axis otherwise intended. Default NULL requires legacy weights = alias (see ...). reporting_support Optional M x J per-unit reporting axis; overrides object$reporting_support weight-list path. NULL (default) takes axis object. Ignored object gp_fit. ids Optional length-J identifiers. NULL (default) takes object$ids, row names W, seq_len(J). Ignored object gp_fit. control Optional gp_control recorded result. NULL (default) uses gp_control() weight-list path. Ignored object gp_fit. assumption Character scalar; pairwise independence assumption recorded source. Defaults "one_level_independence" (one-level analytic path). Ignored object gp_fit. ... Unused, except legacy named weights = alias object; argument raises error. power Integer scalar; matrix-power option. KRW / public path 0L (default) implemented M1 surface; value errors.

title: Pairwise outranking probability matrix — gp_pairwise
text: validated gp_pairwise object (list class c("gp_pairwise", "list")) public slots: ids Character vector length J; canonical unit-id order, row/column names matrix. matrix Numeric J x J matrix outranking probabilities [0, 1]; matrix[, j] = Pr(theta_i > theta_j), diagonal 0.5. power Integer; matrix-power option (0L). cleanup Named list (antisymmetry, diagonal, zero_floor); applied cleanup contract – antisymmetry , diagonal 0.5, one-sided 1e-7 floor. source Named list (stage, rule, assumption); matrix produced independence assumption. control gp_control recorded result. warnings, schema_version, provenance Internal audit slots.

title: Pairwise outranking probability matrix — gp_pairwise
text: compute path entry per-unit CDF integral pi[, j] = sum_m W[, m] * F_j(theta_i[m]^-), F_j unit j's posterior CDF strictly argument (ties contribute 0). Cleanup applied frozen order antisymmetry (pi[j, ] = 1 - pi[, j]) -> diagonal set 0.5 -> zero-floor: every -diagonal value 1e-7 raised 1e-7 (one-sided floor; exact 1 stays exact 1), final antisymmetry exact floor tolerance. See KRW (2024, Section 4) pairwise method vignette.

See gp_m2_status(). strict <- function(n, p) {   id <- paste0(p, seq_len(n)); m <- matrix(0, n, n, dimnames = list(id, id))   for (i in seq_len(n)) for (j in seq_len(n)) if (i != j) m[i, j] <- if (i < j) 0.95 else 0.05   m } pw <- gp_twolevel_pairwise(strict(4, "f"), strict(3, "i"),                            control = gp_control(backend = "highs")) gp_twolevel_report_card(pw, control = gp_control(backend = "highs")) } # }

M2 PARTIAL_ACCEPTED: exact L01 industry grade counts pass; gender continuous L02 rows promoted banded intermediate fixture parity; race continuous L02 rows remain approximate Pi_theta fixture gap 0.0121756787 > 0.01. N10 support rows synthetic evidence guards, registered paper-value passes. M2 PROMOTED / banded rows fixture-parity evidence, direct reproduction paper's industry DR, tau, R2 values. gradepath imports ebrecipe package stage-1 input container, controlled source low-level primitives behind seam R/seam-ebrecipe.R, one-level cross-check oracle.

text: first public pre-release gradepath, native-R replication discrimination-report-card analysis Kline, Rose & Walters (2024, AER). one-level race gender core (M1) accepted predeclared scoped policy, two-level industry surface (M2) partially accepted, release ships full documentation layer: reader-first reference, two-track vignette set, pkgdown site, hex logo, README/NEWS.

title: gradepath 0.5.0
text: Native KRW estimation core, entirely R. full pipeline — beta-GMM precision standardization; empirical-Bayes prior deconvolution posterior shrinkage; pairwise posterior outranking matrix; Bayes-risk grade integer program; information-reliability frontier — now runs R, replacing original Stata + R + Python + MATLAB + Gurobi pipeline. License-free install, load, test. grade integer program solves Gurobi default; open backends HiGHS (direct) ROI plugins (GLPK / SYMPHONY) supported, workflow runs without commercial license. package installs, loads, tests without solver present. M1 (one-level race + gender) accepted predeclared scoped policy. recovers published grade distributions — race 2 / 81 / 14 gender 1 / 3 / 89 / 4 — proven-optimal solves (mip_gap = 0) KRW baseline lambda = 0.25, beta = 0.5095 (race) beta = 1.2554 (gender) matching paper.
